sanskrit: यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् । ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥
hindiTranslation: यह अस्थिर और चञ्चल मन जहाँजहाँ विचरण करता है, वहाँवहाँसे हटाकर इसको एक परमात्मामें ही लगाये ॥
englishTranslation: Whenever the restless, unsteady mind wanders, bring it back under the control of the true self.
essence: The mind is mastered by repeated return, not by force.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Each time the mind runs off, bring it back again and again to the true self.
simpleInsight: Progress comes from returning, not from never drifting.
detailedMeaning: Krishna is still teaching Arjuna on the battlefield at Kurukshetra, where the warrior’s mind must be trained like a wild horse. After telling him to withdraw the mind from distraction, Krishna gives the practical method: every time it escapes, bring it back.
modernRelevance: You open your phone to check one message and lose twenty minutes. The practice is not to shame the drift — it is to notice it, and return.
translationLiteral: यतो यतः (wherever) / निश्चरति (wanders out) / मनः (the mind) / चञ्चलम् (restless) / अस्थिरम् (unsteady) / ततस्ततः (from there, from there) / नियम्य (restraining) / एतत् (this) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / एव (only) / वशम् (under control) / नयेत् (should bring)
